title: gaultheria capitata var. capitata
intro: Gaultheria capitata var. capitata, also known as Mountain Cranberry or Rock Cranberry, is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to the mountainous regions of New Zealand and Australia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of salicylic acid and other organic compounds. The health benefits of Gaultheria capitata var. capitata include its potential to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its use in treating digestive issues and skin conditions. Gaultheria capitata var. capitata can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in traditional medicine to make topical creams and salves for wound healing and skin care.
